input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to `.runsettings` after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me first look at their project files to understand the current setup, then provide guidance.

Let me check their files and also invoke the relevant skill for MSTest migration.

data.content: Your `.testsettings` file is a **legacy format** that MSTest 3.x ignores. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Replaces TestTimeout from .testsettings Properties -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / deployment from .testsettings -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
    <!-- Per-method timeout (milliseconds) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileTracingEngine, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Include>
                <ModulePath>.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Include>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file that configures:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment
2. Test timeout (30000ms)
3. Code coverage data collector

They've upgraded to MSTest 3.8.0. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and not supported by modern MSTest. They need to migrate to `.runsettings`.

Let me invoke the MSTest migration skill for detailed guidance.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Replaces TestTimeout from .testsettings Properties -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / deployment from .testsettings -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
    <!-- Per-method timeout (milliseconds) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileTracingEngine, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Include>
                <ModulePath>.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Include>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Here's what changed and why:

**`.testsettings` → `.runsettings` mapping:**

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` (per-test) and `<TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>` (session-wide) |
| `<AssemblyResolution>` / deployment | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` |
| `<DataCollector uri="...CodeCoverage/1.0">` | Updated to `CodeCoverage/2.0` URI with configuration block |

**To use it**, run:
```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```

Or add to your `.csproj`:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
</PropertyGroup>
```

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` once you've verified the new settings work.
